name: woocommerce-markdown
description: Guidelines for creating and modifying markdown files in WooCommerce. Use when writing documentation, README files, or any markdown content.
capabilities: Validates heading styles, list formatting, and code blocks
Provides markdown error fixes and troubleshooting
Ensures UTF-8 encoding for special characters
Validates CLAUDE.md files for AI parsing
author: woocommerce
securitySummary: This skill is pure documentation containing markdown formatting guidelines. Static findings are false positives: backticks in code examples are markdown syntax, not shell execution; 'MD003/MD007' are linting rule numbers, not crypto algorithms; example URLs are documentation placeholders; relative paths are instructional examples. No executable code, network calls, or file operations exist.
valueStatement: WooCommerce documentation requires specific markdown formatting standards. This skill ensures your markdown files follow WooCommerce linting rules and style guidelines.
limitations: Only applies to WooCommerce project markdown files
Requires markdownlint to be installed locally
Must run from WooCommerce repository root directory
Cannot auto-fix all markdown issues manually

useCases.title: Document new features
useCases.description: Create README files and documentation that meet WooCommerce markdown standards

useCases.title: Format documentation
useCases.description: Ensure all markdown files follow WooCommerce style guide and pass linting

useCases.title: Fix linting errors
useCases.description: Quickly identify and resolve markdown formatting issues in pull requests

promptTemplates.title: Check markdown formatting
promptTemplates.prompt: Check if this markdown file follows WooCommerce guidelines. List any violations and how to fix them.

promptTemplates.title: Format code blocks
promptTemplates.prompt: Review this markdown and ensure all code blocks have proper language specifications.

promptTemplates.title: Fix list formatting
promptTemplates.prompt: Fix the list formatting to use 4-space indentation and proper blank lines.

promptTemplates.title: Create CLAUDE.md
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me create a CLAUDE.md file following WooCommerce markdown guidelines.

bestPractices: Always run markdownlint from the WooCommerce repository root to load proper configuration
Use markdownlint --fix first to auto-correct most formatting issues
Specify language for all code blocks to improve readability and syntax highlighting
antiPatterns: Don't use ASCII art for directory trees - use UTF-8 characters instead
Never skip heading levels (H1 → H3) - maintain proper hierarchy
Avoid using emphasis (bold/italic) as headings - use proper heading tags

faq.question: Why must I run markdownlint from the repository root?
faq.answer: The WooCommerce project has a .markdownlint.json configuration file in the root that defines custom rules.

faq.question: What if markdownlint --fix does not solve all issues?
faq.answer: Some issues like missing language specifications require manual fixes. The linter will indicate what needs attention.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for non-WooCommerce markdown files?
faq.answer: The guidelines are specific to WooCommerce configuration. Other projects may have different markdownlint rules.

faq.question: How do I handle UTF-8 encoding issues?
faq.answer: Use the provided tr command to remove control characters, or ensure your editor saves files in UTF-8 format.

faq.question: What is the difference between MD031 and MD032 errors?
faq.answer: MD031 requires blank lines around code blocks, while MD032 requires blank lines around lists.

faq.question: Are tabs allowed in WooCommerce markdown?
faq.answer: Yes, unlike standard markdownlint rules, WooCommerce allows hard tabs as configured in their settings.
